home.title: Choir of Maritime University of Szczecin
home.description: Official website of the Choir of Maritime University of Szczecin – one of the most acclaimed choral ensembles in Poland, known for exceptional artistic quality and a wide repertoire. Discover our history, achievements, upcoming events and collaborations with distinguished artists.

footer.brandName: Choir of Maritime University of Szczecin
footer.description: An academic choir with a tradition spanning over 20 years. We perform a wide repertoire, from classical to contemporary music.
footer.followText: Follow us so you don't miss any concert

hero.subtitle: under the baton of Prof. Sylwia Fabiańczyk-Makuch

about.body1: For over 30 years we have been creating a space where choral music becomes an unforgettable experience. Our ensemble combines passion, professionalism and an unceasing pursuit of perfection.
about.body2: We have earned recognition on stages across Europe, winning awards at the most prestigious choral music festivals. Every concert is a meeting with an audience that shares our love of harmony.

creativity.heading: Listen to our music
creativity.description: We create music videos, albums, and record in professional studios

conductor.quote: Choral music is the art of breathing together. When dozens of voices become one, magic happens that cannot be described in words.
conductor.body: With an education from the Music Academy in Warsaw and years of experience on European stages, she has led our ensemble to the highest artistic peaks for over 15 years.

support.body: Thanks to the support of our Patrons we can develop our artistic activities, record new albums and perform on international stages. Every contribution brings us closer to our next musical dream.

hero.subtitle: Passion, excellence and the tradition of choral music

story.title: Choir of Maritime University of Szczecin
story.paragraph1Rich: Under the direction of the founder, <highlight>Prof. Sylwia Fabiańczyk-Makuch</highlight>, the choir has been present on the music scene for over 20 years. The ensemble quickly became a hallmark of the city and region, spreading maritime tradition in Poland and abroad.
story.paragraph2: This nearly one-hundred-member group has performed hundreds of concerts, completed over a dozen international tours, and won dozens of top awards at prestigious choral festivals in Poland and internationally.

awards.title: Our awards
awards.descriptionRich: In the last 10 years alone, we have won over <highlight>85 major awards</highlight> including 14 Grand Prix, 50 Gold Medals and numerous special distinctions

touring.title: International tours
touring.description: The ensemble has visited dozens of countries and earned recognition from international audiences

collaboration.title: Collaboration with stars
collaboration.description: The choir is invited to collaborate with outstanding artists from the Polish and international music scene

premieres.title: World premieres
premieres.description: The choir has delivered more than a dozen world-premiere performances of works by renowned Polish composers, including:

expo.title: Expo Osaka
expo.paragraph1Rich: The Choir of Maritime University of Szczecin represented Poland at <highlight>Expo 2025 Osaka Kansai Japan</highlight>, one of the world’s most important meeting points for cultures, ideas and people.
expo.paragraph2: The ensemble was invited by the Polish Pavilion and preparations lasted over a year. The concerts attracted such large audiences that an additional encore performance was added. This distinction highlights the choir’s international standing and its role in promoting Polish culture.

fryderyk.title: Fryderyk nomination
fryderyk.paragraph1Rich: In 2023, the album released in November of the previous year titled <highlight>“The Sound of the Sea”</highlight>, featuring maritime-themed works composed specifically for the Choir of Maritime University of Szczecin by outstanding contemporary Polish composers, received a Fryderyk nomination in the category <highlight>Album of the Year – Choral Music</highlight>.
fryderyk.paragraph2: This distinction confirms both the importance of the project and the ensemble’s highest artistic level.

ambassador.title: Ambassador of Szczecin 2019
ambassador.paragraph1Rich: Four years ago, the choir of the Maritime Academy received the honorary title <highlight>Ambassador of Szczecin 2019</highlight>. The honorary award, granted to people whose achievements contribute to building a positive image of Szczecin in Poland and abroad, is decided by a chapter composed of former and current city presidents and editors-in-chief of selected Szczecin media.
ambassador.paragraph2: It is an official confirmation of the ensemble’s role as a cultural showcase of the city.

project.title: Shared Sounds
project.descriptionRich: For several years we have been creators and originators of the project <highlight>Shared sounds</highlight>, which attracts crowds of fans every year. We combine classical choral sound with a variety of genres and styles - from rock (ØRGANEK), through jazz (Kuba Badach, Dorota Miśkiewicz, Komeda), to ethnic music (Kayah and Kroke) and electronic experiments (Fisz Emade Tworzywo, Mikromusic, Smolik).

awardsPage.title: Our awards
awardsPage.descriptionRich: In the last 10 years alone, we have won over <highlight>85 major awards</highlight> including 14 Grand Prix, 50 Gold Medals and numerous special distinctions

joinUsPage.description: We are looking for people who want to sing, grow musically and co-create an ensemble present on stages in Poland and abroad. If you have sensitivity, energy and a willingness to work in a shared sound, reach out to us.

rehearsals.title: Rehearsals
rehearsals.description: Regular work with the ensemble, continuous craft development and musical discipline without stiffness.

stage.title: Stage
stage.description: Concerts, recordings and ambitious projects that let you truly feel the energy of a shared sound.

ensemble.title: Ensemble
ensemble.description: People connected by sensitivity, commitment and the satisfaction of creating something bigger together.

hero.description: Professor at the Academy of Art in Szczecin, as well as the conductor and founder of the Choir of Maritime University of Szczecin, with whom she has performed for over 20 years and won prestigious awards in Poland and abroad.

biography.title: Biography
biography.description: A conductor, educator and promoter of music life in West Pomerania, combining concert, teaching and organizational work.

education.title: Education and academic degrees
education.contentRich: The I. J. Paderewski Academy of Music in Poznan (Szczecin branch), <highlight>graduated with distinction</highlight> in the class of Prof. Jan Szyrocki. Postgraduate <highlight>Choirmaster Studies</highlight> at the F. Nowowiejski Academy of Music in Bydgoszcz. <highlight>Doctor of Musical Arts</highlight> (conducting) - Fryderyk Chopin University of Music in Warsaw, 2011. <highlight>Postdoctoral degree</highlight> - Academy of Music in Gdansk, 2017.

artistic.title: Artistic activity
artistic.contentRich: <highlight>Jury member</highlight> of choral festivals and reviews. Since 2001, she has been leading <highlight>voice emission training workshops</highlight>. In 2008-2014, conductor of the choir operating at the Secondary Music School Complex in Szczecin. Founder of the vocal ensemble <highlight>Pomerania Singers</highlight> (2015), active in the most important artistic events of the city and region.

educational.title: Educational and organizational activity
educational.contentRich: Together with Pomerania Singers, she released an album with works by Romuald Twardowski (Tryptyk morski) and the Stella Maris Mass op.49 no. 4 by Feliks Nowowiejski. Since 2017, she has been affiliated with the Academy of Art in Szczecin, where she teaches conducting, score reading and methodology of leading vocal ensembles. <highlight>Director of the Academic Culture Center</highlight> at Maritime University of Szczecin. <highlight>Artistic Director of PZChiO</highlight> Szczecin branch and of the Choir's original project <highlight>"Shared sounds"</highlight>.

awards.title: Awards and distinctions
awards.intro: The most important distinctions granted to the conductor for artistic, educational and social activity.

cavalierCross.title: Knight's Cross of the Order of Polonia Restituta
cavalierCross.description: For outstanding contributions to the development and promotion of Polish choral music, especially maritime music, and for charitable activity.

rectorAward.title: Academy of Art in Szczecin - Rector's Award
rectorAward.description: For outstanding artistic achievements in the discipline of musical arts.

proArte.title: "Pro Arte" Award of the Marshal of West Pomerania
proArte.description: For outstanding achievements in artistic creation, dissemination and protection of culture.
